description:
## Problem

`src/favicon_storage.cr` (461 LOC) is the second-largest source file. It contains a `FaviconActor` class with significant DRY violations, dead code from a previous async refactor, a reliability gap in SSRF protection, and a vestigial backward-compat module.

## Categories of Issues

### Reliability (correctness)
- `do_fetch_google_favicon` follows redirects **without SSRF private-host checks** â€” the other two fetch paths both check, this one does not.
- Hardcoded pool key `"true"` in Google fetch error cleanup (line 358) â€” would leak a pooled client if the URL scheme ever changed.
- `valid_image_data?` only rejects HTML/XML content, never validates actual image magic bytes (PNG/JPEG/ICO).

### Dead code (~100 LOC)
- The original async fetch path (`HttpFetchResult` struct, `handle_fetch_and_save` at line 44, `perform_http_fetch`, `handle_http_fetch_result`, `@pending_fetches`) was superseded by the synchronous-in-actor approach at line 240 but never removed. Crystal resolves the duplicate method name by overwriting, so the old code is unreachable.

### DRY (duplication)
- HTTP fetch-with-redirect pattern duplicated **3 times**: `perform_http_fetch` (dead), `handle_fetch_and_save`, `do_fetch_google_favicon`.
- Atomic file write pattern (write .tmp â†’ rename) duplicated twice â€” once with error handling, once without.
- Client pool key construction duplicated in error cleanup blocks with an inconsistency.

### Readability / structure
- `FaviconStorage` backward-compat module (40 lines of pure delegation) is a migration artifact.
- Two methods named `handle_fetch_and_save` with same signature at lines 44 and 240 â€” confusing naming collision.
- `valid_image_data?` name implies validation but it only does a negative check (rejects HTML).

## Strategy

1. Remove dead code first (easy win, ~100 LOC reduction).
2. Fix SSRF gap in Google fetch (correctness).
3. Extract DRY helpers (HTTP fetch with redirect, atomic file write).
4. Update callers to use `FaviconActor` directly and remove `FaviconStorage` wrapper.

## Dependency Graph
```mermaid
graph TD
  EPIC[Refactor favicon_storage.cr]

  DEAD1[P2] -- remove dead async fetch code --> EPIC
  REL1[P1] -- SSRF gap in Google fetch --> EPIC
  REL2[P2] -- hardcoded pool key --> EPIC
  REL3[P2] -- image magic byte validation --> EPIC
  DRY1[P2] -- extract HTTP fetch helper --> EPIC
  DRY2[P3] -- extract atomic file write helper --> EPIC
  READ1[P3] -- remove FaviconStorage wrapper --> EPIC

  DEAD1 --> DRY1
  REL1 --> DRY1
  REL2 --> DRY1
  REL3 --> DRY1
  DRY1 --> READ1
```

## Acceptance
- All child tickets closed
- `just nix-build` passes
- `nix develop . --command crystal spec` passes
- File under ~250 LOC after all cleanup
- All three fetch paths share one HTTP-with-redirect helper
- `grep -c "FaviconStorage" src/*.cr` decreases (wrapper removal)
- No bare rescues remaining
title: [EPIC] Refactor src/favicon_storage.cr (461 LOC) for readability and reliability
